agent_output:
**Bug found and fixed:** `.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties` was missing from the repository.

The `mvnw` script (version 3.3.4) reads this file to determine which Maven version to download. Without it:
- The CI `./mvnw verify -q` step fails immediately — the script exits with `cannot read distributionUrl property`
- The `docker build` step fails — `COPY .mvn/ .mvn/` either errors on the missing directory, and then the `RUN ./mvnw ...` commands inside the build would also fail

**Fix:** Created `.mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties` with the standard `distributionUrl` pointing to Maven 3.9.9 and `wrapperUrl` matching the Maven Wrapper 3.3.4 version declared in the `mvnw` script header.
git_log:
5e71901 fix: add missing .mvn/wrapper/maven-wrapper.properties so mvnw and docker build can resolve Maven
043e08c baseline

agent_output:
**Bug found and fixed:** `pom.xml:32` — the `org.postgresql:postgresql` dependency was declared with `<scope>test</scope>`.

With test scope, the PostgreSQL JDBC driver is available during test execution but is excluded from the Spring Boot fat JAR produced by `mvnw package`. The Docker image built in CI therefore contains an application that cannot connect to PostgreSQL at runtime (no driver on the classpath), causing deployment to fail.

Fix: changed scope to `runtime`, which is the correct scope for a JDBC driver — needed at runtime but not at compile time.
git_log:
fe470dc fix: change postgresql driver scope from test to runtime so it is included in the packaged JAR
b942ea1 baseline

agent_output: The bug was in `pom.xml`: the PostgreSQL JDBC driver was declared with `<scope>test</scope>`, which excludes it from the packaged JAR. At runtime the application can't connect to Postgres because the driver is missing from the classpath — causing CI failures when the Docker image runs. Removing the scope declaration restores the default `compile` scope so the driver is bundled into the artifact.
git_log:
1120919 fix: remove test scope from postgresql driver so it is included at runtime
8fb2fc2 baseline

agent_output:
The bug was in `pom.xml` at line 33: the `postgresql` JDBC driver had `<scope>test</scope>`, which excludes it from the Spring Boot fat JAR. At runtime (in the Docker container), Spring Boot's auto-configuration tries to create a `DataSource` using the `jdbc:postgresql://...` URL from `application.yml`, but fails immediately because no PostgreSQL driver exists on the classpath.

Fixed by changing to `<scope>runtime</scope>` — the driver is now packaged into the production JAR while still being available during tests.
git_log:
fc5410c fix: change postgresql driver scope from test to runtime so it is included in the production JAR
dbcc2ea baseline
